metadata.scene_name: Sliding vs Rolling Down a Curve
metadata.task: Determine which object, the rolling sphere or the sliding cube, reaches the bottom first.
metadata.description: Work from the Sliding vs Rolling Down a Curve scene state rather than from the title alone. Query accessible object parameters and, when the task involves motion, collisions, forces, or oscillation, step the simulator and compare measured states before submitting the numeric answer.

expected_behavior: The LLM shows that the cube (object_2), if sliding with minimal rotational losses, can reach the bottom faster.
reasoning: Rolling objects lose energy to rotation while sliding (if frictionless) converts all potential energy to translational kinetic energy.
